methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
Stegra operates in the Climate & Energy sector at the Growth stage, having raised $7.0B+ to date. The comparable universe in this sector is broad—21 peers identified across public and private markets—though a sufficiently tight peer set at Stegra's stage and valuation tier proved insufficient for reliable median-based triangulation. The narrow peer cohort includes one same-stage and one adjacent-stage company, alongside a single public comparable (LNZA), which falls short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold for peer-median methodology.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was the applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($7.0B) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier associated with Growth-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $8.4B. This reflects the capital already deployed and a modest premium for execution risk reduction at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7.1B to $9.7B, with a median point of $8.4B. This range is anchored to a single methodological input and should be interpreted accordingly. Key uncertainties include the adequacy of total-raised-as-proxy for fair value, the applicability of standard Growth-stage multipliers to frontier-tech capital intensity, and the absence of market-tested comps at Stegra's specific scale. Additional institutional or market data would materially sharpen conviction in this range.
